$schema_note: Maps a source to a confidence tier 0-3. Use the HIGHEST matching tier. The issuer/attorney is never above tier 0 for its own claims.

tiers.label: Primary government / court record
examples: USCIS approved Regional Center list
USCIS Regional Center termination / debarment list
USCIS I-956F / I-956 / I-956G data or approval/denial notice
SEC EDGAR filings (S-1, D, litigation releases, AAERs)
SEC enforcement / litigation releases and orders
PACER / federal court dockets and judgments
State court records and judgments
U.S. Census Bureau ACS data (for TEA)
Bureau of Labor Statistics LAUS unemployment data (for TEA)
County recorder / register of deeds (liens, deeds, UCC filings)
Municipal building / permitting department records
OFAC / sanctions lists

tiers.label: Reputable independent third party
examples: Established trade / financial press (e.g. national newspapers, recognized real-estate and immigration trade outlets)
Independent market-study and absorption data from a recognized firm
Third-party audited financial statements (auditor is independent of sponsor)
Independent fund administrator confirmations
Recognized EB-5 industry data services / litigation trackers
Ratings agencies and independent analysts

tiers.label: Single weak / uncorroborated third party
examples: A single blog, forum post, or press release not from the issuer but uncorroborated
Aggregator listings with unknown provenance
Issuer claim that has exactly one weak corroborating signal
Marketing material from a partner/affiliate of the issuer

tiers.label: Issuer-only / none
examples: The Private Placement Memorandum (PPM)
The I-526E exemplar provided by the issuer
The issuer's business plan or economic report (as a claim source)
The issuer's / regional center's marketing website and brochures
The immigration attorney's summary or recommendation
No source found at all
tiers.note: Anything here makes a claim UNVERIFIABLE unless an independent tier >= 2 source corroborates it.
